justification: As the Monsun operator, A is materially more useful. Coverage: A surfaces the 2026 'Labor Law Revolution' — PIP's new authority to administratively convert civil/B2B contracts, civil-contract tenure now counting toward 26-day paid vacation, banned unpaid trials, and 30,000 PLN fines for missing salary ranges — none of which B mentions, despite all being live employer liabilities right now. Specificity: A names seven specific Świętojańska competitors (Sumo Ramen, Umiko Sushi, Czerwony Piec, Panda Wok, Ogniem i Piecem, Sushi Poke Go, Oficyna Gdynia) and ties each to a contract strategy; B names zero competitors and stays generic. Source diversity: A mixes government (gov.pl, pomorskie.eu), industry reports (Devire, MADE FOR Restaurant), regional press (trojmiasto.pl, wyborcza.pl) and job boards across 47 domains; B is largely job-board mono-source. Actionability: A gives Monsun a decision tree on contract type, scheduling software (Kadromierz), tip-psychology training, and a flagged retention advantage worth defending. Specialist set is a TIE — A added market_landscape but it materially helped; neither was wrong.
spot_check_a: A claims the 2026 minimum wage is 4,806 PLN brutto / 31.40 PLN brutto hourly — internally consistent with Polish 2026 statutory rates and matched by B. Cited contract-tenure rule (civil/B2B time counting toward staż pracy from Jan 1 2026) is a real legislative change attributable to gov.pl/infor.pl drawer sources. Competitor-to-contract pairings (Sumo Ramen on zlecenie, Umiko Sushi on UoP, Czerwony Piec on B2B) are claims that olx.pl/pracuj.pl listings can plausibly support.
spot_check_b: B's core PIT-0 claim (students under 26 on umowa zlecenie are exempt from PIT and ZUS, making gross = net) is factually accurate Polish tax law. The 31.40 PLN gross hourly floor matches A and is consistent with pip.gov.pl. The 150 PLN/shift tip pool figure echoes A's claim but lacks the named-venue grounding A provides.
